full_prompt: Design the upper link of the manipulator arm for an IRC 2026 planetary rover. Per the IRC 2026 Rulebook, the rover + arm mass must not exceed 65 kg, total mass with payload must not exceed 85 kg, and the rover must fit within a 1.5 m x 1.2 m footprint. The manipulator arm is a 6-DOF serial kinematic chain designed to pick and place up to 5 kg at 1.2 m reach (shoulder to tool tip). This spec covers the 'upper link' connecting shoulder pitch joint to elbow pitch joint. Geometry: the link is a straight closed-box aluminium weldment, cross-section 60 mm x 40 mm external with 3 mm walls, length 600 mm between pivot centers. Each end has a reinforced boss to accept a double-row ball bearing of 30 mm OD, 10 mm ID, 9 mm wide (for a D6010Z / equivalent). The shoulder end has a 4-bolt pattern (M5, 30 mm square) for attaching a harmonic drive gearbox flange; the elbow end has an identical 4-bolt pattern. Material: welded 6061-T6 aluminium (Young's 69 GPa, Poisson 0.33, density 2700 kg/m^3, yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa; assume welded HAZ properties reduced to 138 MPa yield / 165 MPa UTS in a 25 mm band around each weld). Loads: static 5 kg payload at tool tip with arm extended 1.2 m horizontal; gravity on Mars (3.71 m/s^2) for in-mission analysis, but on Earth during test (9.81 m/s^2) for proof. Load cases: LC1 (Earth test at full extension) — 5 kg x 9.81 = 49 N downward at elbow end, plus the weight of the link and all joints beyond, roughly 30 N additional, plus a 4 Nm shoulder reaction moment. With shoulder end fully fixed and elbow end loaded: apply 80 N vertical + 4 Nm bending moment about the strong axis; LC2 (Mars operational) — 19 N vertical + 1.5 Nm (scaled by Mars gravity); LC3 (Emergency stop deceleration) — arm moving at 0.5 m/s tool speed, abrupt stop in 100 ms: apply 2 g inertial load at the tool tip = 100 N at elbow; LC4 (Impact on stow) — 30 g lateral shock during rover landing or hard traverse (rover mass = 65 kg, arm stowed); apply 30 g lateral on the link mass = 30 * 9.81 * 0.8 kg (link mass estimate) at link mid-span; LC5 (Thermal) — Mars day-night cycle: -80 C to +20 C uniform, with both ends fully fixed (captures worst case when rover is parked and thermally cycled). Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises in base material under LC1, LC3, LC4 < 184 MPa (yield/1.5); (R2) max von Mises in HAZ region under same LCs < 92 MPa (HAZ yield/1.5); (R3) tip deflection under LC1 < 2.5 mm (so tool-tip placement accuracy is preserved); (R4) first-mode linear buckling under LC4 compression >= 3.0; (R5) link mass < 850 g so arm total mass budget of 8 kg is preserved; (R6) under LC5 combined thermal + gravity (Earth), max stress < 184 MPa in base and < 92 MPa in HAZ. Primary verification: linear static structural FEA for LC1-LC4 and thermal-structural coupled for LC5; linear buckling for LC4. Vibration modal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required (the ionising-radiation budget for a rover skin is addressed in other deliverables, not in this link). Output one STEP AP242 single-body link plus a report listing link mass, max stress in base vs. HAZ per case, tip deflection, buckling factor, and combined thermal stress.
short_prompt:
Design the upper link of the 6-DOF manipulator arm of an IRC 2026 planetary rover per IRC 2026 Rulebook (rover + arm <= 65 kg, total with payload <= 85 kg, footprint <= 1.5 x 1.2 m). Arm picks/places up to 5 kg at 1.2 m reach; this link connects shoulder-pitch to elbow-pitch.

Envelope: welded closed-box 6061-T6 aluminum, 60 x 40 mm external x 3 mm walls x 600 mm pin-to-pin; both ends carry 30 mm OD bearing bosses and 4 x M5 harmonic-drive flange patterns on a 30 mm square. HAZ band 25 mm wide around each weld with reduced allowables (138 MPa yield / 165 MPa UTS).

Factor regime: yield FoS 1.5 on base metal, 1.5 on HAZ; linear buckling eigenvalue >= 3.0 on the impact case; tip accuracy <= 2.5 mm under Earth test.

Load cases.
- Earth test at full extension: 80 N vertical + 4 N-m bending at the elbow end (includes link + downstream-joint self-weight).
- Mars operational (3.71 m/s^2): 19 N + 1.5 N-m.
- Emergency stop: tool-tip at 0.5 m/s decelerating in 100 ms = 100 N lateral at elbow (2 g).
- Landing / traverse impact: 30 g lateral on the link mass, applied at mid-span.
- Thermal: Mars day-night -80 C to +20 C uniform, both ends fixed.

prompt.title: IRC 2026 rover arm upper link
prompt.brief: Welded 6061-T6 box-section upper arm link, 600 mm pivot-to-pivot, for a 65 kg IRC rover arm lifting 5 kg at 1.2 m reach across Earth test and Mars ops with HAZ weld penalty.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Launch vibe not a rover arm concern on IRC (earth-launched by vehicle truck).
exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Out of scope for a structural link; handled in rover skin spec.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Cold-weld avoidance in Martian CO2 atmosphere

eval_coverage: welded structure with explicit HAZ penalty zone
multi-gravity operational spec (Earth proof + Mars ops)
